message:
fix(semantic): warn on NULL created_at during semantic backfill

R2 review finding: src/indexer/semantic.rs:embedding_input_from_row silently
defaulted a NULL `created_at` to 0 (Unix epoch), making the row
indistinguishable from a legitimately-1970 message in downstream code that
reads `created_at_ms`:

  - src/search/vector_index.rs range predicates
    (parsed.created_at_ms < from / > to) — the row would either be
    always-excluded (from > 0) or always-included (from = 0, masquerading
    as the oldest possible message).

  - stable_hit_hash (src/search/query.rs:4091) — the row's hash is
    computed from a bogus timestamp.

Previous code gave NO operator signal when this happened. Now we emit a
structured `tracing::warn!` carrying the message_id so NULL-created_at
rows surface in logs instead of being discovered by puzzling over 1970
timestamps in semantic hits. The defaulting behavior itself is preserved
(the row still gets indexed, just with a known-bogus timestamp) — the
fix is the visibility, not the data path.

Not filing a bead: this is small, the fix is obvious, and the defaulting
behavior is probably deliberate (dropping the row would miss content in
semantic search).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
